title: 10 best herbal capsules for fibrocystic breast disease
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for fibrocystic breast disease are a natural and effective supplement that helps alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. They contain a blend of herbs such as Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica sinensis, Cimicifuga racemosa, Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Trifolium pratense, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Silybum marianum, Dioscorea villosa, and Urtica dioica, which work together to reduce breast tenderness, inflammation, and hormonal imbalances. If left untreated, fibrocystic breast disease can cause significant discomfort, anxiety, and impact daily activities, leading to emotional distress, decreased productivity, and strained relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, swelling, and pain. The capsules also support the body's natural detoxification processes, helping to eliminate excess hormones and toxins that may contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, dandelion root has been shown to improve liver function, which can help to regulate hormone metabolism and reduce symptoms associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the breasts, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain, tenderness, and swelling associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Choleretic and bile-stimulating effects: Dandelion capsules contain compounds that stimulate bile production and release, which may help improve liver function and promote the excretion of excess estrogen and toxins from the body, reducing the risk of fibrocystic breast disease.

Lymphatic system support: Dandelion has been traditionally used to support lymphatic circulation and drainage, which may help reduce swelling, pain, and tenderness in the breasts by removing toxins and excess fluids from the body.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly taraxasterol and inulin, may help alleviate fibrocystic breast disease symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating hormone levels.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in dandelion, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate breast pain, swelling, and other symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, including terpinen-4-ol and borneol, found in dandelion may also contribute to its potential benefits for fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and promoting hormone balance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to help reduce water retention in the body, which may contribute to fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their potential to act as a natural diuretic and help reduce fluid retention in the breasts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in late morning on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground taraxacum officinale powder with a natural capsule filling agent such as cornstarch in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill the mixed taraxacum officinale powder into empty capsules using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with cysts. The herb also possesses estrogen-balancing properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to fibrocystic breast symptoms. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals, promoting overall breast health and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation. By supporting healthy breast tissue and hormonal balance, stinging nettle capsules offer a natural solution for managing fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polysaccharides and polyphenols, reduce inflammation in the breasts, which can alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Estrogen-balancing properties: Stinging nettle contains flavonoids that help regulate estrogen levels, which is beneficial for women with fibrocystic breast disease who may have hormonal imbalances contributing to their condition.

Antioxidant and free radical-scavenging properties: Stinging nettle's antioxidant compounds protect the breasts from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease by inhibiting the growth of cysts and mitigating hormonal imbalances.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Urtica dioica have antioxidant properties, which help protect breast tissue from oxidative stress and potentially reduce the severity of fibrocystic symptoms like breast pain and tenderness.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic acid found in Urtica dioica, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and promoting overall breast health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Roots: Contain diosmin, which helps reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness and swelling.
Stems: May be used due to their high levels of histamine and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Dry 250 grams of urtica dioica leaves in a low temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 5 grams of cornstarch and 5 grams of cellulose powder.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain mucilages that soothe and calm irritated breast tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The gel-like substance in marshmallow root also helps to coat and protect the breasts, allowing for easier lymphatic drainage and reduced fluid buildup. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce pain and tenderness associated with fibrocystic breast disease. By promoting a healthy and balanced breast environment, herbal marshmallow capsules can provide natural relief from fibrocystic breast symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that can help reduce inflammation in the breasts, which is often a hallmark of fibrocystic breast disease.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants like vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and polyphenols that can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage in the breasts, reducing the risk of cyst formation and pain.

Demulcent effects: The mucilage present in marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the skin and mucous membranes, which may help soothe and calm irritated breast tissue, reducing discomfort and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce the growth of cysts and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Althaea extract's galactomannans and galacturonic acid: Galactomannans and galacturonic acid have a regulatory effect on estrogen levels and hormone balance, which can help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing estrogen-related inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe breast discomfort.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Stems: Also contain mucilages and other beneficial compounds that may help alleviate breast pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the roots in a temperature-controlled environment at 40°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 200 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 8 grams of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal powder mixture and seal them to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules help with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain beta-glucans, a type of soluble fiber that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of beta-glucans help to soothe swollen breasts, ease pain, and regulate hormonal imbalances that can exacerbate fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, oats have antioxidant properties that protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall breast health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Oat capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease, such as pain and swelling.

Saponins: Saponins in oat capsules have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect the breast tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the severity of symptoms.

Phenolic acids: The phenolic acid content in oat capsules has been found to have anti-cancer and anti-tumor properties, which may help prevent the progression of fibrocystic breast disease and reduce the risk of developing more severe conditions.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease by counteracting oxidative stress and modulating hormonal balance.

Saponins: These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing breast tenderness and discomfort by inhibiting the growth of cysts and promoting overall hormonal equilibrium.

Fatty acids (including oleic and linoleic acid): These essential fatty acids help regulate hormone production, improve blood circulation, and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of fatty acids, specifically gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), which may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Roots: They are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce symptoms such as breast pain and swelling.
Stems: They are used due to their potential to regulate hormonal balance, which may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 500 grams of avena sativa roots and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested avena sativa roots and leaves for about 2 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried avena sativa roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 200 grams of the powdered avena sativa with 100 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of water in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the avena sativa mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a plant compound called casticin, which has been shown to help alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness and pain. The capsules work by balancing the body's hormonal levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which are often imbalanced in individuals with fibrocystic breast disease. By regulating these hormones, chaste tree capsules can help reduce breast swelling and cysts, leading to relief from uncomfortable symptoms and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects: Chasteberry's flavonoids and terpenes help to reduce inflammation and dilate blood vessels, which can alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, swelling, and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Hormone-regulating properties: Chasteberry has been shown to inhibit the activity of prolactin, a hormone that can contribute to breast lump formation and other symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease, promoting a healthy balance of hormones in the body.

Antioxidant and anti-estrogenic effects: Chasteberry's antioxidants help protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive estrogen levels, which is thought to contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly aucubin and agnuside, help to alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by regulating hormonal imbalances and reducing prostaglandin production, which contributes to breast pain and tenderness.

Flavonoids: Vitex agnus castus contains flavonoids such as vitexin and isovitexin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the breast tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Diterpenoid Ursolic Acid: Ursolic acid, a diterpenoid present in Vitex agnus castus, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to reduce breast pain and discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Seeds: The seeds contain the highest concentration of ursolic acid, which may help to regulate hormonal imbalances associated with the condition.

Leaves: They are also a significant source of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to the therapeutic effects of Vitex agnus castus capsules in treating fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vitex agnus castus berries during the plant's flowering stage.
Grind 50g of the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 150g of gelatin powder in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 50ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir for 10 minutes to dissolve.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb, Calendula officinalis, has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with the condition. The capsules' active compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of cysts and nodules, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, tenderness, and lumpiness. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold capsules neutralize free radicals that can damage healthy tissue, reducing the risk of inflammation and cell damage, which is common in fibrocystic breast disease.

Hormone-regulating effects: Pot marigold has been shown to have a mild estrogen-like effect, which may help regulate hormone imbalances that contribute to fibrocystic breast disease symptoms such as breast tenderness, lumps, and pain.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid, may help with fibrocystic breast disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in breast tissue, ultimately alleviating breast pain and discomfort.

Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce breast tenderness and alleviate symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating properties, which help to balance hormone levels and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 30c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 5g of the powdered flowers with 95g of gelatin and 5g of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a gelatin capsule filler and 500 capsules per hour.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Rosavin is a phenolic compound found in Rosa x rugosa, and it may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and modulating hormonal imbalances.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, in Rosa x rugosa may help with fibrocystic breast disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to breast cyst formation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of rosa x rugosa flowers and leaves on a sunny day after the morning dew has evaporated.
Dry the collected material in a single layer on a paper bag or a food dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried rosa x rugosa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any large particles and obtain a smooth texture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-300 milligrams of the powder and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable source in late summer or early fall.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle in 5-10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 30-40 grams of gelatin capsules and 10-20 grams of starch in a bowl.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon in 10-15 minutes.
Label and store the herbal glycyrrhiza glabra capsules in a cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a natural compound called petasin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce the swelling and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, petasin is able to block the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can exacerbate breast tenderness and lumpiness. By reducing inflammation and blocking prostaglandins, butterbur capsules may help alleviate symptoms and provide relief for women suffering from fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Butterbur's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and swelling in the breasts, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain, tenderness, and lumpiness associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Antioxidant activity: Butterbur's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the breasts, potentially reducing the severity of fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.

Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Butterbur's ability to inhibit COX enzymes helps reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like compounds that can cause inflammation and pain in the breasts, thus providing relief from fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and modulating the hormonal balance in the body.

Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): These compounds may help reduce mastalgia (breast pain) and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

Petasins (specifically, petasin and isopetasin): These sesquiterpene lactones may help reduce prostaglandin levels, which contribute to breast pain and inflammation in fibrocystic breast disease, thereby providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Petasites hybridus are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Petasites hybridus are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their ability to reduce pain and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Petasites hybridus are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their hormone-regulating properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest petasites hybridus roots in autumn after the first frost when the tops have withered and died.
Dry the roots in a warm dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with a binding agent like gelatin or plant-based cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of matricaria chamomilla flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 90 grams of starch in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb silymarin, found in milk thistle, has been shown to reduce the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause breast tenderness and swelling associated with fibrocystic breast disease. As a result, taking milk thistle capsules may provide relief from symptoms and promote overall breast health.

Flavonoids: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of flavonoids in milk thistle capsules may help to reduce cyst size, improve breast texture, and relieve discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds from mature plants, ensuring proper drying and storage for later use.
Grind 20 grams of dried silybum marianum seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered silybum marianum with 100 grams of tapioca starch and 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl, allowing for uniform blending.
Fill #00 capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of the powdered silybum marianum blend and 250 milligrams of glycerin, sealing each capsule manually or using an automated capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dark place for up to 24 months, ensuring proper labeling and packaging for future use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of cirsium arvense at the recommended dosage of 500 milligrams per day.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered flower with 100 milliliters of water to create a standardized extract and stir for 15 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the standardized extract and seal tightly for storage and dosing purposes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems in the summer or early fall ensuring they are free of pesticides and contaminants.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 1-2 hours until it reaches a brittle texture.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes to ensure thorough processing.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered hypericum perforatum with 100 milligrams of silica gel in a small bowl for each capsule, and fill empty capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to one year or until they are completely consumed for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a blend of herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These herbs, such as turmeric and ginger, help to reduce the inflammation and swelling associated with fibrocystic breast disease, which can cause pain, tenderness, and lumps in the breasts. Additionally, the antioxidants in these herbs may help to protect the breast tissue from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall health and well-being of the breasts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal corn capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the breasts, which are common symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in these capsules can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the breasts, potentially alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Stimulating lymphatic drainage: Some herbal corn capsules contain ingredients that can stimulate lymphatic circulation, which can help remove toxins and excess fluid from the breast tissue, reducing cysts and other symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Barks: Contain compounds like anthraquinones, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried zea mays kernels from a reputable supplier.
Grind the zea mays into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 3 minutes.
Mix the ground zea mays powder with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan to form a paste.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat and reduce heat to simmer for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and let it cool before filling capsules with 500 milligrams each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate hormonal imbalances. Lavender's calming properties can help soothe and calm the body's response to inflammation, which is often associated with fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, lavender's estrogen-balancing effects may help regulate hormone levels, reducing symptoms such as tenderness, swelling, and lumpiness. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal English lavender capsules offer a natural and holistic approach to managing fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin and apigenin, can help reduce inflammation in the breasts, which is often a characteristic symptom of fibrocystic breast disease.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant compounds may help protect breast tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease.

Pain-relieving properties: Lavender has been shown to have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease, such as breast tenderness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit estrogen-modulating effects, potentially helping to alleviate symptoms of the condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for capsule production.
Sieve dried flowers through a 40-mesh sieve to separate finer particles from larger debris.
Mix 250mg of sifted lavender flowers with 50mg of glycerin and 100mg of gelatin powder.
Encapsulate the mixture using a #00 capsule filling machine with a target fill volume of 300mg.
Store capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: South American yerba capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. The capsules' active compounds, including chamomile and peppermint, work together to soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, yerba's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to fibrocystic breast disease, promoting overall health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yerba Mate contains polyphenols and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease such as pain, swelling, and tenderness.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in Yerba Mate capsules, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease.

Hormone-balancing compounds: Yerba Mate contains saponins, triterpenes, and other phytochemicals that have been shown to exhibit estrogen-like effects, potentially helping to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease, such as breast tenderness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Buds: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the breasts, reducing discomfort and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achyrocline satureioides leaves and stems in quantities of 50 grams.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind the dried achyrocline satureioides into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground herb with 250 milligrams of gelatin and fill into capsules using an encapsulation machine within 30 minutes.
Store the capsules in a cool, dry place protected from light and moisture for up to 6 months or longer if refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a unique blend of herbs that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these herbs, such as turmeric and ginger, help to reduce swelling and pain in the breasts, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Additionally, the capsules' ability to regulate hormonal imbalances can help to mitigate the symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease, leading to a significant improvement in overall breast health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lime capsules contain antioxidants such as limonoids, flavonoids, and vitamin C, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the breasts, alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsular material of herbal limes, particularly the flavonoids and terpenes, exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Hormone-balancing properties: Herbal lime capsules contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to regulate hormone levels, particularly estrogen, which can help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate breast pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help relieve symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease such as breast tenderness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia europaea flowers at dawn in june when they are fully bloomed and dry them immediately.
Grind 30 grams of dried tilia europaea flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground tilia europaea flower powder with 60 grams of acacia fibre and 10 grams of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill 1000mg vegetable capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency for up to 2 years.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain bioflavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and tenderness in the breasts. Hawthorn's ability to relax the uterine muscles also helps to reduce cramping and discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant properties may help to protect against cellular damage and promote overall breast health, leading to a reduction in symptoms and an improvement in quality of life for women affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to the reduction of inflammation and fibrosis associated with Fibrocystic Breast Disease.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate breast pain, tenderness, and swelling caused by Fibrocystic Breast Disease.

Vasodilatory Properties: Hawthorn's bioactive compounds may help relax blood vessels and improve circulation to the breasts, reducing pressure on cysts and potentially alleviating symptoms such as lumpiness, heaviness, and nipple discharge.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to treat breast complaints, including fibrocystic breast disease, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and pain.

Barks: The barks of the hawthorn plant contain flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in mid-summer for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind dried crataegus monogyna into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 1 minute.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered crataegus monogyna with 500 milliliters of vodka in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth into another container and fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract per capsule.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fibrocystic breast disease that are treated with herbal capsules are those affecting the reproductive and hormonal systems. These may include symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding, painful menstruation, and irregular periods. Herbal supplements like Dong Quai, Red Clover, and Black Cohosh are commonly used to alleviate these symptoms by balancing hormone levels and regulating menstrual cycles. Other herbs like Ginger and Turmeric may also be used to reduce inflammation and relieve associated pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with fibrocystic breast disease is a blend of Chasteberry, Fenugreek, and Wild Yam. Chasteberry helps to regulate hormonal imbalances, while Fenugreek reduces inflammation and alleviates symptoms. Wild Yam provides relief from cramping and cystic pain due to its estrogen-balancing properties. These herbs work synergistically to address the root causes of fibrocystic breast disease, promoting hormonal balance and reducing symptoms. Consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements to ensure safe and effective use.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Menopause (New York, N.Y.), herbal capsules containing isopropanolic black cohosh extract (Remifemin Menopause) may be beneficial for fibrocystic breast disease due to its estrogen-antagonistic effects and lack of proliferative effects on estrogen-sensitive breast cancer cells. The study used an MCF-7 cell culture model to evaluate the effects of various herbal menopause preparations on estrogen-sensitive breast cancer cells. The results showed that isopropanolic black cohosh extract did not stimulate MCF-7 growth, indicating a favorable safety profile for use in women with a history of breast cancer, which may include those with fibrocystic breast disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen dominance, which is often associated with the condition. The capsules contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the pituitary gland to produce more progesterone, counterbalancing the effects of estrogen. This hormonal balance can reduce breast tenderness, cysts, and inflammation, alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus capsules may also help to normalize menstrual cycles and reduce PMS symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate fibrocystic breast disease by regulating hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen levels, which contribute to breast pain and cysts.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Vitex agnus-castus capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with fibrocystic breast disease.

Phenylethylamine (PEA): PEA, a phenolic compound, helps alleviate fibrocystic breast disease by modulating neurotransmitters and hormones, such as prolactin and serotonin, which play a role in breast pain and tenderness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their potential to regulate hormonal imbalances.
Seeds: The seeds are used in capsule form to potentially alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by affecting hormonal activity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers from a reliable supplier within 1-2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground powder with 200 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Add 10 drops of glycerin and 1 gram of magnesium stearate to the bowl and mix for 1 minute.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active constituents in Angelica sinensis, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area. This can help to alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease, including breast tenderness and pain. Additionally, the capsules may help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often a contributing factor to the condition.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: Helps with fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in breast tissue, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling.

Ligustilide: Contributes to the relief of fibrocystic breast disease by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce breast tenderness.

Butylphthalide: Aids in the management of fibrocystic breast disease by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can contribute to pain and inflammation in the breasts, and also by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their high content of danggui, which has anti-inflammatory and estrogen-balancing properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their ability to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with estrogen imbalance.

Fruits: The fruits of Angelica sinensis are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease due to their high content of ferulic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Dry the angelica sinensis root in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or starch in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.
Label and store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because it has anti-inflammatory and hormone-regulating properties. The active compounds in Black Cohosh, such as actein and 23-acetylactein, have been shown to reduce breast tenderness and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease. By balancing estrogen and progesterone levels, Cimicifuga racemosa capsules may alleviate symptoms like lumps, swelling, and breast discomfort. This natural remedy may provide relief for women experiencing fibrocystic breast disease, promoting overall breast health and well-being. Regular use may lead to improved symptoms and a better quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Rotenone: May help to reduce symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate breast tenderness.

Caffeic acid: Plays a role in reducing symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids, which can help to alleviate breast pain and tenderness.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of the plant's medicinal compounds, including ferulic acid and cimicifugoside, which are believed to alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Seeds: They are also used in herbal supplements for fibrocystic breast disease, although less commonly than roots and rhizomes, and are believed to have similar therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried cimicifuga racemosa roots and store them in a cool dry place for future use.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2g of the ground powder with 10g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 50 empty capsules with the powder mixture for 10 minutes.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce the inflammation associated with fibrocystic breast disease, alleviating symptoms such as breast tenderness and swelling. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have a balancing effect on hormones, which can contribute to the development of the condition. By reducing inflammation and hormone imbalances, Zingiber officinale capsules may provide relief for women suffering from fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Shogaols: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the size and number of cysts, and alleviate symptoms such as breast pain and tenderness.

Volatile oils (zingiberene): May help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to the development of fibrocystic breast disease, by modulating the body's response to estrogen and progesterone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger are also used due to their similar composition to rhizomes, making them a viable alternative for some formulations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from a well-drained soil with sufficient sunlight.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed rhizomes at 40 degrees celsius in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mill with a 40-mesh sieve.
Fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the zingiber officinale powder each.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce breast tenderness and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease. Curcumin has also been found to have a regulatory effect on the hormonal imbalances that contribute to the condition. By reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance, Curcuma longa capsules may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease, providing relief and improving overall breast health.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are associated with the condition.
Tumerones: They help with fibrocystic breast disease by acting as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents, which alleviate symptoms such as breast pain and swelling.

Volatile oils (specifically, sesquiterpenes): They help with fibrocystic breast disease by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce breast tenderness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a compound called curcumenol, which has been found to have anti-estrogenic effects that can help reduce the severity of fibrocystic breast symptoms.

Roots: They are used as they contain a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried turmeric root powder and transfer it to a mixing bowl.
Add 20 grams of starch and mix well to prevent clumping for 2 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the mixture and seal them tightly.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight.
Check the capsules for quality and expiration after 12 months of storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain the herb Red Clover, which is rich in isoflavones. These isoflavones have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease such as breast tenderness and swelling. Additionally, Red Clover's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the breasts. By promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation, Trifolium pratense capsules may provide relief from fibrocystic breast disease symptoms, promoting a more comfortable and healthy breast.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These flavonoids help alleviate fibrocystic breast disease by potentially reducing estrogen dominance and improving hormonal balance in the body.
Saponins: These compounds may help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by modulating the body's hormone-sensitive cells and tissues.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fibrocystic breast disease, thereby alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules due to their isoflavone content, which may help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules as they contain a high amount of isoflavones, specifically isoflavones genistein and daidzein, which may provide relief from fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules as they are rich in isoflavones, which may help reduce symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried trifolium pratense flowers at 2 pm on a sunny day in july.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 3 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a small bowl for 1 minute.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate breast tenderness. The flavonoids present in glycyrrhiza glabra may also help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often a contributing factor to fibrocystic breast disease. Additionally, glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the breast tissue from oxidative damage and promote overall health and well-being. remedy_constituents: Licorice saponins: These saponins may help alleviate fibrocystic breast disease symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating hormone levels, thereby alleviating breast pain and discomfort.

Glycyrrhetic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-androgenic properties, which can help reduce breast tenderness and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease by blocking the effects of excess androgens on breast tissue.

Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in breast tissue, leading to alleviation of symptoms such as breast pain, swelling, and tenderness.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe breast tissue and reduce pain.
Seeds: They are a rich source of saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic effects that can help to alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and wash them thoroughly with water.
Dry the washed roots in a well-ventilated area at a temperature of 50-60 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gum arabic in a ratio of 1:2 and add water to make a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the paste and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes liver health. The liver plays a crucial role in detoxifying hormones, including estrogen, which can contribute to fibrocystic breast disease. By supporting liver function and reducing hormone buildup, silymarin helps alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, cysts, and painful menstrual cycles. Additionally, silymarin's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce breast swelling and discomfort associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: It helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in breast tissue, which may alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce breast tissue sensitivity and alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness and pain.
Silychristin: It has anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating effects, which may help reduce breast tissue growth and alleviate symptoms associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in silymarin, which is believed to help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and improving hormonal balance.

Leaves: Containing various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which may contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in treating fibrocystic breast disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather the necessary materials including silybum marianum seeds 50g dried flowers 20g and empty gelatin capsules 100 count.
Dry the silybum marianum flowers in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried silybum marianum flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the silybum marianum powder with a binding agent such as cornstarch 2g and fill the mixture into the gelatin capsules.
Seal the capsules by hand or using a capsule filling machine and store them in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dioscorea villosa capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain diosgenin, a plant-based steroid that mimics the effects of estrogen in the body. This can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to fibrocystic breast disease. The capsules also contain anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce breast tenderness and swelling associated with the condition. Additionally, diosgenin has been shown to promote cell health and reduce the growth of cysts, making it a promising natural remedy for managing fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate breast tenderness by regulating hormone levels and improving lymphatic circulation.

Steroids: Steroids in Dioscorea villosa capsules may help to reduce breast inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by modulating hormone production and improving hormone balance.

Furostanols: Furostanols may help to regulate estrogen metabolism and alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease by reducing the formation of estrogen metabolites that can contribute to breast inflammation and tenderness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dioscorea villosa roots are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease because they contain diosgenin, a compound that helps to regulate hormonal imbalances.
Rhyzomes: Dioscorea villosa rhyzomes are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease because they also contain diosgenin, which can help alleviate symptoms of the condition.
Stems: Dioscorea villosa stems are used to make capsules for fibrocystic breast disease because they contain a higher concentration of diosgenin, making them a valuable source for herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried dioscorea villosa roots to be used in this recipe.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 teaspoon of a natural binder and 1/2 teaspoon of glycerin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a spoon and a capsule filling machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica capsules helps with fibrocystic breast disease because they contain nettle root extract, which is rich in sterols and lignans. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of hormone-sensitive breast tissue, reducing symptoms such as breast tenderness and swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties of nettle root may also help to reduce inflammation in the breast tissue, providing relief from fibrocystic symptoms. Additionally, nettle root has been found to have a balancing effect on hormone levels, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Help with fibrocystic breast disease by reducing inflammation and improving the balance of hormones, which can alleviate symptoms such as breast pain and tenderness.
Flavonoids: Aid in the treatment of fibrocystic breast disease by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate breast pain.

Polysaccharides: Assist in the management of fibrocystic breast disease by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as breast pain and tenderness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of fibrocystic breast disease.
Roots: Roots are utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with fibrocystic breast disease.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, beneficial in managing fibrocystic breast disease symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems with scissors when the plant is in full growth stage about 2-3 feet tall.
Dry the harvested plant material in a dehydrator at 40°c for 2-3 hours to prevent degradation and spoilage.
Grind the dried urtica dioica plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 500mg of the ground plant material with 500mg of a filler such as starch or cellulose in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal content.
